description_long: This is a well-used screwdriver featuring a hexagonal wooden handle with visible wear and discoloration, suggesting frequent use. The handle is robust and blocky, providing a solid grip, and is attached to a long, metallic shaft. The shaft is straight and tapers to a flathead tip, suitable for slotted screws. The wood of the handle shows signs of aging, with scratches and a faded finish, while the metal shaft appears slightly tarnished but intact. No brand markings or logos are visible on any surface. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on function over aesthetics.
description: A worn, hexagonal wooden-handled screwdriver with a flathead metal tip and visible signs of use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the screwdriver, highlighting the hexagonal wooden handle and straight metal shaft.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, displaying its hexagonal shape and the transition to the metal shaft.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the handle and shaft, with the flathead tip more visible.
description_view_3: View from the tip end, focusing on the flathead blade and the end grain of the wooden handle.
attribute_handle_type: hexagonal, straight, wooden, rough surface, no ergonomic contouring
attribute_visible_wear_or_damage: Visible scratches, discoloration, and surface wear on the wooden handle; metal shaft slightly tarnished.
